# @cloud/ui

> The shared React design system for Cloud applications. **Read before you write:** [Layout](foundations/layout.md) picks the page shell; then the pattern for that page type; [Action weight](patterns/action-weight.md) before you place a button; [Delete patterns](patterns/delete-patterns.md) before any delete. **Never hardcode a user-facing string** — add the key to the `en` base.

## Get Started

What you can import, from where, and in what order the stylesheets load.

- [API surface](api-surface.md): **Every importable name and the entry point it comes from** — plus the CSS import order, and the hooks, theme helpers, and utilities (`cn`, `useTheme`, `useIsMobile`, `useInfiniteScroll`, `useListFilters`, `SidebarProvider`) that have no component doc of their own. Charts are a deliberate exception: they live only at `@cloud/ui/components/chart`, never the root barrel and never `recharts`.

## Components

Each entry states the condition that selects it, and names the component to use instead when it does not. Open a component doc for its props.

- [Accordion](components/accordion.md): Several sections that expand independently, pre-styled with chevrons and dividers. For a single unstyled show-and-hide region, use `Collapsible`.
- [Action footer](components/action-footer.md): The commit band at the bottom of a form or task **page**. Put the Cancel/primary pair here and they stay in view on short and long forms alike — it sits below the scroll region and never scrolls, so it needs no `sticky`. It is a **sibling of `PageBody`, never its child — and it never goes inside a `Modal`**, where the dialog's own footer owns the commit.
- [Advanced filter](components/advanced-filter-sheet.md): `AdvancedFilterSheet` (with `AdvancedFilterGroup` / `AdvancedFilterField`) — the right-side sheet for filter fields that do not fit the quick bar. **The quick bar holds search plus one or two high-traffic `Select`s; every filterable property beyond that — a date window, a reference code, a further category — moves into the sheet.** A filter lives in exactly one place, never both. **`AdvancedFilterField` is not a `Field`**, so a `Select` inside it needs an explicit `className="w-full"` or its box resizes to the chosen value. Deferred apply — nothing filters until the user presses Apply.
- [Advanced filter button](components/advanced-filter-button.md): The quick bar's trigger for that sheet, badged with the number of active advanced conditions.
- [Alert](components/alert.md): A **persistent, in-page** status message. Not a toast and not a modal: for a transient confirmation use `toast`; for a blocking forced choice use `AlertDialog`.
- [Alert dialog](components/alert-dialog.md): The **forced-choice** dialog — no close affordance and no backdrop dismiss, so the user must decide. Esc **does** still close it — the Base UI primitive keeps that keyboard escape hatch, and it is the accessible behaviour — so treat Esc as cancel and never hang the destructive branch off the close path. **It is the confirmation dialog for every delete tier that has one**. An async confirm drives `open` itself and puts a plain `Button` in the footer, because `AlertDialogAction` closes on click. For a dialog the user may simply close — a form, a preview — use `Modal`.
- [App header](components/app-header.md): The app bar's content — leading slot, breadcrumbs, search, notifications. One per shell, inside `Layout`.
- [Aspect ratio](components/aspect-ratio.md): Pin child media to a fixed width-to-height ratio. Make the child fill it (`<Image fill>` or `size-full`); do not size the child separately.
- [Avatar](components/avatar.md): A person or entity as an image with an initials fallback; `AvatarGroup` overlaps several. Pass `name` and let it derive the initials.
- [Badge](components/badge.md): A status, count, or category chip. **Colour comes only from `tone`** — if no tone carries the meaning, stop and raise it rather than reaching for an arbitrary colour utility.
- [Breadcrumb](components/breadcrumb.md): The low-level parts, for a bespoke trail you assemble by hand. For the app-shell trail, use `Breadcrumbs`.
- [Breadcrumbs](components/breadcrumbs.md): The app-shell breadcrumb — takes typed items, renders into `AppHeader`'s slot. **This is the one you want** unless the trail is bespoke.
- [Button](components/button.md): Every action. **The variant follows the slot, not the verb** — read [Action weight](patterns/action-weight.md) before choosing one. `loading` also disables, so no separate double-submit guard is needed.
- [Calendar](components/calendar.md): The bare month grid. Use it alone **only when the grid is the page** — an availability view, a schedule. To enter a date in a form, use `DatePicker`.
- [Card](components/card.md): A bordered surface grouping related content. `elevation` sets its depth.
- [Card strip](components/card-strip.md): Pick **one entity** from a horizontal strip of cards — a store, an account, a project — above the view it scopes. Past `toolbarThreshold` items it grows a filter/count/arrows toolbar and hoists Add out of the row. For slides you page through rather than pick from, use `Carousel`; when the entity has no card face worth showing, use `Select` or `Combobox`.
- [Carousel](components/carousel.md): Slides you **page through**. To *pick one entity* from a row of cards instead, use `CardStrip`.
- [Chart bar](components/chart-bar.md): Themed bars, including stacked shapes and per-cell colour.
- [Chart container](components/chart.md): The wrapper every chart needs — config, responsive sizing, semantic series colours, shared context. **Import every chart part from `@cloud/ui/components/chart`, never from `recharts`** — its `Tooltip` and `Legend` collide with `@cloud/ui`'s own, which is why charts are kept out of the root barrel.
- [Chart legend](components/chart-legend.md): `ChartLegend` + `ChartLegendContent`, optionally clickable to toggle series. **Never Recharts' `Legend`.**
- [Chart pie callout](components/chart-pie-callout.md): Labels and connector lines on pie or donut sectors, when the slices are too thin to label inline.
- [Chart sparkline](components/chart-sparkline.md): A trend with no chart chrome — for a stat tile or a table cell, where a full chart would not fit.
- [Chart states](components/chart-states.md): The standard chart loading and empty states. A chart with no data is not an empty page.
- [Chart tooltip](components/chart-tooltip.md): `ChartTooltip` + `ChartTooltipContent`. **Never Recharts' `Tooltip`** — it is unthemed, and importing it collides with `@cloud/ui`'s own `Tooltip`.
- [Checkbox](components/checkbox.md): Independent boolean options, with an indeterminate state for a "select all" header. **For a standalone setting use the `ToggleCheckbox` recipe; inside a form use `Field` + the bare `Checkbox`.** `size="sm"` (12px) belongs only in a data-table select column.
- [Collapsible](components/collapsible.md): Headless show-and-hide of one region — you own all the styling. For a grouped, pre-styled set of sections, use `Accordion`.
- [Combobox](components/combobox.md): A searchable dropdown, single or multi-select. **Prefer it over `Select` as soon as the list is long enough that scanning it is work** — a country, a merchant, a region. **It is also the multi-select control**: pass `multiple` and `value` becomes a `string[]`, the picks render as removable chips in the trigger, and `maxChips` caps how many show.
- [Command](components/command.md): The keyboard command palette. Use `CommandDialog` for the modal form, or `Command` bare when the palette lives inside a panel or a popover.
- [Content header](components/content-header.md): The header family's **shared title block** — title, description, flush-right actions. It has no band chrome, so it is **never a page header on its own**: it is composed inside `PageHeader` / `PageHeaderBand`, or used as a section title inside `PageBody`. For a level-1 list page use `PageHeader`; for every other page use `PageHeaderBand`.
- [Context menu](components/context-menu.md): Actions from a right-click on a region. For actions from a button, use `DropdownMenu`.
- [Date picker](components/date-picker.md): A single-date field — an input-styled trigger opening a calendar popover. The default way to enter a date.
- [Date range picker](components/date-range-picker.md): A date interval, with optional presets beside the range calendar.
- [Date-time picker](components/date-time-picker.md): A date and a time as one field. Use it only when both halves genuinely matter; otherwise use `DatePicker`.
- [Drawer](components/drawer.md): The **mobile** bottom sheet, with drag-to-dismiss. For a desktop side panel use `Sheet`; for a centered task use `Modal`.
- [Dropdown menu](components/dropdown-menu.md): Actions from a button — roving focus, type-ahead, submenus, checkbox and radio items. For a right-click menu use `ContextMenu`; for a plain floating panel use `Popover`.
- [Dropzone](components/dropzone.md): Drag-and-drop file **picking** — it emits the picked `File[]` through `onFiles` and **uploads nothing**, makes no network call; the transport is yours. Render the files with **`FileList` + `FileRow`**, which carry the per-file `status` (`pending`/`uploading`/`done`/`error`), a `progress` bar, and `onRemove`. Don't hand-roll the row, and don't reach for `Progress`.
- [Empty](components/empty.md): The zero-data placeholder inside a table, list, or panel. **Which message it carries depends on why it is empty** — read [Empty states](patterns/empty-states.md) before writing the copy.
- [Field](components/field.md): Wraps a control with its label, hint, required mark, and error. **Every form control belongs in one** — a placeholder is not a label.
- [Hover card](components/hover-card.md): A rich preview on hover. For a short text hint use `Tooltip`; for content the user clicks to open, use `Popover`.
- [Input](components/input.md): Validation tones beyond the invalid state.
- [Input group](components/input-group.md): One unified border around an input plus its addons, units, or compact buttons.
- [Input OTP](components/input-otp.md): Segmented entry for a one-time passcode or verification code.
- [Key value](components/key-value.md): Read-only labelled properties on detail and overview pages — **`KvGrid` is the container and `KeyValue` the cell**. Never hand-write the auto-fit grid; the arbitrary-value lint blocks it. An empty value renders an em dash on its own.
- [Label](components/label.md): The accessible label for a control. When it cannot be visible, keep it and hide it visually — never remove it.
- [Layout](components/layout.md): The page shell — sidebar, app header, and the `<main>` everything else lives in. **Nothing in the shell scrolls: `<main>` clips, and the page's scroll root is `PageBody`.** One per app.
- [List applied filters](components/applied-filters.md): `AppliedFilters` — the row of applied filter chips, with a "clear all".
- [List condition band](components/list-condition-band.md): The list page's filter band — the quick bar over the applied-chip row. Leave `sticky` off on a standard list page; dock the summary bar and table header to `PageBody`'s top edge instead.
- [List filter chip](components/filter-chip.md): `FilterChip` — one applied filter, with a remove button.
- [List filter state](components/use-list-filters.md): The `useListFilters` hook — the **draft/applied** state machine every list page's filters run on. Read it before wiring the band or the sheet: `setDraft` is `(key, value)`, the list queries `applied` and never `draft`, and `reset` (draft only) is not `clearAll` (draft **and** applied).
- [List search input](components/search-input.md): `SearchInput` — the quick bar's search field. Enter runs the search.
- [List summary bar](components/list-summary-bar.md): The result count and low-chrome actions between the card's top edge and the table. **Every tool verb and neutral bulk verb here is `ghost`; a destructive bulk verb is `ghost-danger`** — never an outlined button, which draws a box inside a box. See [Action weight](patterns/action-weight.md).
- [Load more](components/load-more.md): Append-on-click pagination. **When anything sits below the list, use this rather than scroll-driven loading**. For scroll loading, use `VirtualTable`'s `onReachEnd` or the `useInfiniteScroll` hook.
- [Log console](components/log-console.md): A dense, level-coloured log viewer with a filter toolbar. **It owns its own scrolling viewport — bound it with `maxHeight` (default `24rem`) and never wrap it in a `ScrollArea`.** For chronological business events, use `Timeline` instead.
- [Menubar](components/menubar.md): A desktop-style application menu bar. Rare in a web console — for actions under one button, use `DropdownMenu`.
- [Modal](components/modal.md): A centered, blocking child task subordinate to the current page. Use it for **one compact, focused action** — for creation, about one simple primary field; for edit, one property or a small tightly related group. `Sheet` is not its larger size; it is for supplementary page content. A forced decision uses `AlertDialog`.
- [Navigation menu](components/navigation-menu.md): Horizontal navigation with rich dropdown panels. The app's own nav lives in `Sidebar`.
- [Object tile](components/object-tile.md): The square identity mark for a company, app, or device model — a glyph, name-derived initials, or a short code.
- [Page body](components/page-body.md): The padded content column of a page — and **the page's one scroll root**. All page content goes through it, or it is clipped and unreachable. **It owns the page padding and the block gap** — never hand-write them. A tabbed detail page gives **each panel its own `PageBody`**; `PAGE_BODY_PADDING_CLASS_NAME` is padding only, creates no scroll root, and is for the rare host that must own the padding itself.
- [Page header](components/page-header.md): The full-bleed band for **level-1 pages** only — a list, an index, a dashboard, a settings root. Title, description, `HeaderAction[]` actions (`icon` **required**, no `size`), and **no back button**, because a level-1 page is a navigation root. The base page surface's primary action lives here. Every page you drill *into* — create, edit, detail, sub-page — uses `PageHeaderBand`.
- [Page header band](components/page-header-band.md): The full-bleed band for **every level-2/3 page** — create, edit, and detail. Prop-driven (not arbitrary children), with a **built-in, non-omittable back button**. `variant="page"` is for create/edit; the `variant="detail"` identity band adds an avatar, an inline status beside the title, a meta row, and tabs on its bottom edge — pass a **plain** `TabsList`, and the band suppresses its line for you. **There is no `sticky` prop**: the bands sit outside `PageBody`, so they never scroll and stay in view for free. Its `actions` take the same **`HeaderAction[]`** descriptors as `PageHeader` — `{label, icon, to?/onClick?, variant?}`, **not JSX, and `icon` is required**.
- [Pagination](components/pagination.md): Bare page-number navigation. **Most list and table footers want `RichPagination` instead**, which adds rows-per-page and a "showing X–Y of Z" summary.
- [Popover](components/popover.md): A floating panel anchored to a trigger. For a keyboard-navigable action menu use `DropdownMenu`; for a hover preview use `HoverCard`; for a short text hint use `Tooltip`.
- [Progress](components/progress.md): Completion when you **know** the percentage. When you do not, use `Spinner` or `Skeleton`.
- [Radio group](components/radio-group.md): One choice from a visible set. **For a standalone setting use the `ToggleRadioGroup` recipe; inside a form use `Field` + the bare `RadioGroup`.**
- [Resizable panels](components/resizable.md): A draggable split-pane layout. Compose `ResizablePanelGroup` + `ResizablePanel` + `ResizableHandle` — **there is no bare `Resizable` export.**
- [Rich pagination](components/rich-pagination.md): The full list/table footer — rows-per-page, range summary, page navigation. **The default footer for a server-paginated collection.**
- [Scroll area](components/scroll-area.md): A **bounded** overflow region — a menu, a long list in a card. **Give it a height or it does nothing.** Never wrap the page itself — `PageBody` is already the page's one scroll root, and a second one nested inside it breaks sticky bars and headers. **Never nest one inside another scroll region** — and note that components which already own a viewport (`LogConsole`) must not be wrapped in one.
- [Select](components/select.md): **One** value from a **short, closed** list. **When the user would rather type to narrow it, use `Combobox`**; for two or three options worth showing at once, use `RadioGroup` or `ToggleGroup`; **for multi-select use `Combobox multiple`** — reach for checkboxes only when every option must stay visible at once.
- [Semantic tone](components/semantic-tone.md): The neutral / success / warning / error / info vocabulary shared by every status-bearing component. Read it before inventing a status colour.
- [Separator](components/separator.md): A divider line. **The `label` form is a different component in disguise** — it renders plain `div`s and silently drops `className`, `orientation`, and every other prop you pass. Put spacing on a wrapper, and reach for `label` only when you actually want the text.
- [Sheet](components/sheet.md): Supplementary content relevant to the current page — advanced filters, page-scoped preferences, or optional supporting information. It may be interactive and scroll, but it is **not a wider Modal or a larger resource editor**. Use a page when the task itself is the destination. This is the closest local counterpart to a Cloudscape drawer; the local `Drawer` is mobile-first.
- [Sidebar](components/sidebar.md): The app's left navigation, with a collapsible icon rail. Read [Side navigation](patterns/side-navigation.md) for hierarchy, current state, collapsed behaviour, mobile parity, and permission-aware visibility.
- [Sidebar trigger](components/sidebar-trigger.md): Collapses the rail on desktop, opens the drawer on mobile.
- [Skeleton](components/skeleton.md): Reserve the layout while content loads. Render skeletons **inside** the real container — a `Card`, a table cell — never in place of it.
- [Slider](components/slider.md): A numeric value or range along a track. **There are two change callbacks and they are not interchangeable:** `onValueChange` streams during the drag — use it for the live read-out only. **Commit on `onValueCommitted`**, which fires once at the end; committing on `onValueChange` writes once per pixel of drag, and neither callback raises a type error when you pick the wrong one. **When the precise number matters rather than the rough position, use `Stepper`.**
- [Spinner](components/spinner.md): A compact indeterminate wait (`role="status"`). **Inside a button, use the button's own `loading` prop instead** — it supplies the spinner.
- [Stat card](components/stat-card.md): One metric, with an optional trend and description. Can double as a quick filter. **Lay a row of them out with `StatGrid`** — its `cols` defaults to 4 and `className` cannot override it, which fails silently. Also exported as `KpiTile`.
- [Status card](components/status-card.md): A header-less card for repeating status lists — media, title, badge, description, and a control footer.
- [Step indicator](components/step-indicator.md): Progress through a fixed sequence of steps. Pairs with the [create wizard](patterns/create-wizard.md) — and if you do not have a wizard, you do not need this.
- [Stepper](components/stepper.md): A numeric input with `−` and `+`. Use it when the value is **small, bounded, and adjusted rather than typed** — a quantity, a retry count, a page size. **`min` and `max` default to ±infinity, so set them** — bounding is not automatic, and an unbounded stepper never reaches a boundary.
- [Switch](components/switch.md): A setting that takes effect **immediately**. For a value that only applies when the form is saved, use `Checkbox`. For a standalone setting use the `ToggleSwitch` recipe; inside a form use `Field` + the bare `Switch`.
- [Table](components/table.md): The typed data table, driven by a `columns` config — never a hand-written `thead`/`tbody`. **Row navigation is `onRowClick`, and `Table` appends the trailing chevron cell itself; inline per-row verbs go in the `rowActions` render prop and share that same cell.** Never write an actions or chevron column into `columns` — you will get two chevrons, and lose the `stopPropagation` that keeps a verb from firing the row. **`onRowClick` does not make the row keyboard-reachable**, so when a row navigates, also put a real link in one of its cells. **For thousands of rows use `VirtualTable`.** Mark every money and count column `numeric` so the digits align.
- [Tabs](components/tabs.md): Switch between peer panels on the same surface. **When the control sets a value rather than navigating between panels, use `ToggleGroup`.**
- [Textarea](components/textarea.md): A character counter when `maxLength` is set.
- [Theme toggle](components/theme-toggle.md): Switches between the light and dark themes. Its label names the **next** state, not the current one.
- [Time picker](components/time-picker.md): A localized time value on its own. For a date and a time together, use `DateTimePicker`.
- [Timeline](components/timeline.md): Chronological business events with actors, times, and statuses — device history, audit trails, ticket activity. For raw, levelled log lines, use `LogConsole`.
- [Toast](components/toaster.md): A **transient, non-blocking** confirmation, via `Toaster` + `toast`. **It disappears, so nothing important may live only there:** for a message that must stay on the page use `Alert`, and for something the user must acknowledge use `AlertDialog`. Never report an actionable failure through `toast.error` alone.
- [Toggle](components/toggle.md): One press-toggle button with an on/off state.
- [Toggle controls](components/toggles.md): The inline-labelled `ToggleCheckbox`, `ToggleRadio`, and `ToggleSwitch` recipes. **These are for a standalone setting** — an inline label beside the control, no hint and no validation. **A control that belongs to a form takes `Field` + the bare primitive instead; never nest a recipe inside a `Field`**, as the two labels fight.
- [Toggle group](components/toggle-group.md): A segmented control, a mode picker, or a set of filter chips. **When the group navigates between panels rather than setting a value, use `Tabs`.**
- [Tooltip](components/tooltip.md): A short text hint on hover or focus. For rich content use `HoverCard`; for content the user interacts with, use `Popover`.
- [Virtual table](components/virtual-table.md): The windowed table for large row counts, with optional scroll loading. Shares `Table`'s columns/sort/rowKey contract. For scroll loading on a list that is not a table, use `useInfiniteScroll`.

## Patterns

Each entry states the condition that selects it. Read the conditions here and pick the branch before opening a pattern.

- [List page](patterns/list-page.md): Use when the page's job is to find, compare, and act on many records. The default collection shape — page header, filter band, applied chips, summary bar, typed table, empty state, and rich pagination.
- [Advanced filtering](patterns/advanced-filtering.md): Use when the fields do not fit the quick filter bar — grouped, rare, or numerous conditions. Adds a right-side sheet with deferred apply; the quick bar alone stays in the list page.
- [Create form](patterns/create-form.md): The dedicated single-page shape for simple to medium creation — roughly 2–15 primary fields or up to five setting groups. About one simple field may use `Modal`; long, complex, or interrelated creation uses [Create wizard](patterns/create-wizard.md). `Sheet` is only for a supplementary sub-task, not the middle size.
- [Create wizard](patterns/create-wizard.md): Use for long or complex configurations and interrelated tasks — roughly more than 16 primary fields or five setting groups, concepts that need their own pages, dependency, branching, step-level recovery, review, or external processing. Otherwise use the create form.
- [Edit resource](patterns/edit-resource.md): Use for **any** edit of an existing record — the create form's fields, pre-filled and dirty-tracked. Several properties, especially interdependent ones, use a dedicated page; one property or a small tightly related group may use `Modal`. `Sheet` is only for ancillary page-scoped configuration, never the larger edit option. There is no inline edit or split-board edit. **The dirty gate is computed on equality, not on "the user has typed"** — the same flag arms the unsaved-changes guard. State transitions — lock, reset password, delete — are actions, never form fields.
- [Secondary panels](patterns/secondary-panels.md): Use for features or information that support the current page but are not essential to its primary task. The supported desktop shape is a right-side `Sheet`, closest in purpose to a Cloudscape drawer but implemented here as a focus-owning dialog. Do not use it as a larger edit modal or persistent selected-resource details panel; this system has no split-panel pattern.
- [Unsaved changes](patterns/unsaved-changes.md): **Required on every create and edit form, in a page or an overlay.** Compute dirty state by value equality and route every in-product exit through the same leave dialog. In an overlay, guard the escape button, close affordance, and `Esc`; on a page, include the header back action, breadcrumbs, sidebar, Cancel action, and automatic navigation.
- [Detail page](patterns/detail-page.md): Use when a single record is the subject of the page. Header band, tabs, key-value overview, related sections, metadata, and permission-aware actions.
- [Delete patterns](patterns/delete-patterns.md): **Start here before building any delete.** The tier follows the blast radius, computed per record at render time — not per resource type. The same button is a different tier on a different row.
- [One-click delete](patterns/one-click-delete.md): No dialog; _Undo_ in the toast. Use **only** when both hold: the blast radius is the record alone, and the completed action can be reliably reversed during the undo window. An irreversible action requires confirmation instead.
- [Delete with confirmation](patterns/delete-with-confirmation.md): One `AlertDialog`, confirm live on open. **The default delete tier** — the record cannot be recreated with a click, but destroying it changes nothing outside itself. A role nobody holds; a pending invitation.
- [Delete with additional confirmation](patterns/delete-with-additional-confirmation.md): The same `AlertDialog` as tier 2, but the confirm stays `disabled` until the user types the record's name — **the gate is the tier**, not a different dialog. Use when the delete reaches **past** the record — other rows cascade, or other people lose permissions. A role eleven users hold.
- [Data visualization](patterns/data-visualization.md): Use when the page must show quantity, trend, or proportion. Chart composition through the themed `@cloud/ui/components/chart` surface, semantic token colors, and standard chart states.
- [Action weight](patterns/action-weight.md): Read before placing any button. **The variant follows the slot, not the verb, and each active surface has at most one `primary`.** The base page and an open modal are separate interaction contexts, so each may retain its own primary; the topmost surface is the active one.
- [Empty states](patterns/empty-states.md): Read when a collection renders zero rows. Three different states with three different recoveries — first-run empty, filtered zero-results, and no-access.
- [Errors and validation](patterns/errors-and-validation.md): Read when anything can fail. Choose field, form, local action, toast, or page-level feedback from the user's next step; actionable errors persist and failed submissions preserve the user's work.
- [Interactive surfaces](patterns/interactive-surfaces.md): Read when a surface responds to the pointer. Hover, selected, navigational, selectable, row-action, and clickable-card rules.
- [Permission gating](patterns/permission-gating.md): Read when an action, content block, or destination is not available to everyone. Hide inaccessible capabilities, disable unmet visible conditions, remove empty chrome, and use a dedicated access-denied surface.
- [Side navigation](patterns/side-navigation.md): Read when adding or arranging a navigation destination. Three-level hierarchy, L2 icons, current state, collapsed behaviour, mobile parity, visibility, and accessibility.
- [Timestamps](patterns/timestamps.md): Read when a time is rendered or entered. Choose the control from the required precision, format for the viewer, make timezone context clear, and choose absolute or relative display deliberately.

## Foundations

The shared visual rules and core principles behind every `@cloud/ui` component and pattern.

- [Design tokens](foundations/design-tokens.md): The CSS-variable → Tailwind-utility → component-prop chain. **Consume the key (`bg-surface-2`), never the value (`bg-[oklch(...)]`)** — the value is allowed to change under you, and the arbitrary-value lint blocks it anyway.
- [Colors](foundations/colors.md): Read before writing any colour. **There is no literal colour at a call site** — surface, content, line, status, category, and chart all have semantic roles. `content-disabled` means disabled; never borrow it to look subtle.
- [Spacing](foundations/spacing.md): Read before writing any gap or padding. **Every step says where it is used**, and the half steps (`gap-1.5`, `gap-2.5`) carry meaning: they say two things are one thing.
- [Typography](foundations/typography.md): Read before writing any text class. Geist; `text-md` (14px) is the body default and **`text-xs` (12px) is the smallest rung there is — there is no 10px**. Each rung carries its own line-height, so you write the size and nothing else. Three rungs are lint errors: `text-sm` and `text-2xs` (deprecated aliases of `md` and `xs`) and `text-base`. **Never `font-mono`** — use `font-code` or `font-chart`; for digits, `tabular-nums`.
- [Elevation](foundations/elevation.md): Depth comes from the shadow ladder and `Card`'s `elevation`. **Never a page-local shadow value**, and never a decorative shadow to fake hierarchy that a surface or line token should carry.
- [Theming](foundations/theming.md): How light/dark activates and how the locale and theme reach the tree. **Consume tokens by role and the dark theme needs no branch from you** — a `dark:` variant for something the tokens already switch is a bug.
- [Motion](foundations/motion.md): Durations and easing come from tokens. **Never type `duration-[180ms]`** — the lint blocks it and it would survive a reduced-motion preference. Don't animate `width`/`height`/`top` when a transform would do.
- [Responsive layout](foundations/responsive-layout.md): Keep responsive rendering in **CSS** — breakpoint variants when the viewport changes composition, `@container` when a component must answer to its own width. **`useIsMobile` is behaviour state, not a rendering switch**; don't render a mobile and a desktop tree from JavaScript.
- [Iconography](foundations/iconography.md): Read before rendering any icon. The set is **lucide**, one set only. **Inside a control you write no size** — `component-defaults.css` makes every unsized `svg` 14px across 24 interactive slots, which is why it must load *before* `globals.css`. Standalone icons are `size-4`. A status icon's colour is owned by the `tone`, never by you. Every icon-only button needs an `aria-label` naming the record — nothing lints it.
- [Accessibility](foundations/accessibility.md): Start from the semantic primitive, then give it a name. **Never remove a focus ring, never encode meaning in colour alone, and never replace a button with a clickable `div`.** An icon-only action needs a specific `aria-label` — a tooltip is not an accessible name.
- [Layout](foundations/layout.md): **Read before composing any page.** The page type picks the shell — a table maps list / create / wizard / detail / dashboard to its header, its body, and the demo to start from. `PageBody` is **the page's one scroll root** and owns the page padding and block gap, so you almost never write either; the header bands and `ActionFooter` are its **siblings**, never its children, and take no `sticky` prop. The sidebar is one tree — `Layout` already renders it in a `Sheet` on mobile.

## Demo walkthroughs

[Index](demos/index.md). These are **prose, not code** — there is no snippet here to copy; runnable code lives in `components/*.md`, next to the component it exercises. Each demo is a shorter restatement of one pattern, and **the pattern is the authority**.

- [List page](demos/list-page.md) → [List page](patterns/list-page.md)
- [Advanced-filter list](demos/advanced-filter-list.md) → [Advanced filtering](patterns/advanced-filtering.md)
- [Create form](demos/create-form.md) → [Create form](patterns/create-form.md)
- [Create wizard](demos/create-wizard.md) → [Create wizard](patterns/create-wizard.md)
- [Detail page](demos/detail-page.md) → [Detail page](patterns/detail-page.md)
- [Charts](demos/charts.md) → [Data visualization](patterns/data-visualization.md)
